meta.purpose: Guide agents on proper unit test isolation patterns for services with external dependencies

overview.title: Unit Test Isolation Patterns
overview.description: Enterprise patterns for isolating unit tests from external infrastructure (Redis, API servers, databases). These patterns ensure fast, reliable test execution without requiring running services.
applicability: Tests for services that connect to Redis
Tests for services that make HTTP requests to other services
Tests for services that connect to databases
Tests expecting specific data structures from implementations

patterns.name: Environment Variable Clearing Pattern
patterns.problem: Service constructors check for environment variables (e.g., REDIS_URL) and create connections if present. This causes unit tests to timeout waiting for unavailable infrastructure.
patterns.solution: Clear infrastructure environment variables in beforeEach BEFORE instantiating the service.
affectedServices: Any service with: if (process.env.REDIS_URL) { this.redis = new Redis(...) }

patterns.name: MSW HTTP Request Isolation
patterns.problem: Tests for services that make HTTP requests to other services (e.g., API Gateway) fail when those services aren't running.
patterns.solution: Use MSW (Mock Service Worker) to intercept HTTP requests and return mock responses.
notes: Use onUnhandledRequest: 'warn' to see which endpoints need mocking
Add handlers for all endpoints your service calls
Reset handlers between tests for isolation

patterns.name: Test-Implementation Alignment Pattern
patterns.problem: Tests expect different data structures than implementations provide (e.g., expecting raw numbers when objects with properties exist).
patterns.solution: Update tests to match actual implementation structure. Verify by reading source file before writing/updating tests.

codeExample.after:
// Test matches actual structure: { role: 'Field Worker', tokens: 1000, includes: [...] }
expect(TOKEN_BUDGETS[0].tokens).toBe(1000);

guidelines: Always read the source file before writing tests for it
Match test expectations to actual exported types/interfaces
When structure differs, update test not implementation (unless implementation is wrong)

patterns.name: Missing Utility Function Pattern
patterns.problem: Tests import functions that don't exist in the implementation yet.
patterns.solution: Add the missing functions to the implementation following existing patterns.
guidelines: Follow existing URN patterns in the codebase
Maintain consistency with related functions (buildRagChunkUrn, buildRagCollectionUrn, etc.)
Add to the end of the relevant section for easy discovery

antiPatterns.name: Testing Against Missing Infrastructure
antiPatterns.problem: Running tests that require Redis/API servers when they're not running
antiPatterns.symptom: Tests timeout after 5000ms with connection errors

antiPatterns.name: Mocking Too Low
antiPatterns.problem: Mocking internal implementation details instead of external boundaries
antiPatterns.symptom: Tests pass but don't test real behavior
antiPatterns.solution: Mock at the boundary (env vars, HTTP requests) not internal methods

antiPatterns.name: Shared Test State
antiPatterns.problem: Tests modifying environment variables without cleanup
antiPatterns.symptom: Tests pass individually but fail when run together

successMetrics.ciExecution: Unit tests must pass in GitHub Actions without Docker services
